perturbation.original_text: Subject to the satisfactory completion of the Company's customary background check, the Board shall appoint the ValueAct Designee to serve as a director beginning within ten (10) calendar days following receipt of the ValueAct Group's written request to the Company to have the ValueAct Designee appointed to the Board; provided that, at such time, the Board does not conclude in good faith and based on the written opinion of outside legal counsel that such appointment would constitute a breach of the directors' fiduciary duties.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to the satisfactory completion of the Company's customary background check, and at the discretion of the Board, the ValueAct Designee may be considered to serve as a director beginning within ten (10) calendar days following receipt of the ValueAct Group's written request to the Company to have the ValueAct Designee appointed to the Board; provided that, at such time, the Board does not conclude in good faith and based on the written opinion of outside legal counsel that such appointment would constitute a breach of the directors' fiduciary duties.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states 'the Board shall appoint'. The changed text states 'ValueAct Designee may be considered to serve' introducing an element of discretion where previously there was an obligation. The original Section 1(a) states that subject to the provided that, the board 'shall' appoint the ValueAct Designee, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the satisfactory completion of the Company's customary background check, and at the discretion of the Board, the ValueAct Designee may be considered to serve as a director beginning within ten (10) calendar days following receipt of the ValueAct Group's written request to the Company to have the ValueAct Designee appointed to the Board; provided that, at such time, the Board does not conclude in good faith and based on the written opinion of outside legal counsel that such appointment would constitute a breach of the directors' fiduciary duties. If the ValueAct Designee is appointed to the Board pursuant to this Section 1(a), the Company shall include the ValueAct Designee as a Class I Director, which term expires at the Company's 2016 annual meeting of stockholders.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies 'the Board shall appoint,' indicating a mandatory action, whereas the changed text states the 'ValueAct Designee may be considered to serve,' introducing discretion where previously there was an obligation. The contradiction lies in the conflicting requirements of the text, where one indicates a guaranteed outcome and the other is discretionary.

perturbation.original_text: The Company agrees that it will seek board or appropriate committee approval of all stock-based awards made to the ValueAct Designee so that the grant of such awards shall be exempt from Section 16(b) of the Exchange Act by virtue of Rule 16b-3 thereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company will consider seeking board or appropriate committee approval of stock-based awards made to the ValueAct Designee so that the grant of such awards shall be exempt from Section 16(b) of the Exchange Act by virtue of Rule 16b-3 thereunder.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states 'The Company agrees that it will seek' making it an obligation, whilst the changed text states 'The Company will consider seeking' turning the obligation into something that may be considered. Changing 'The Company agrees that it will seek' to 'The Company will consider seeking' weakens the commitment to obtain board approval for stock awards, creating an in-text contradiction when the rest of Section 8 still implies such approval is actively pursued.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The ValueAct Designee shall participate in all director compensation and benefit programs in which the Company's other non-employee directors participate. The Company acknowledges that pursuant to the ValueAct Group's policies, cash, equity awards and other property received by the ValueAct Designee are held by such person for the benefit of certain members of the ValueAct Group. The Company will consider seeking board or appropriate committee approval of stock-based awards made to the ValueAct Designee so that the grant of such awards shall be exempt from Section 16(b) of the Exchange Act by virtue of Rule 16b-3 thereunder. Without limiting the foregoing, the Company also acknowledges that as a result of the ValueAct Designee's service on the Board, members of the ValueAct Group may be considered directors of the Company by deputization under applicable interpretations of Section 16 of the Exchange Act. The Company agrees that it will seek board or appropriate committee approval for purposes of Rule 16b-3 for all transactions in classes of Company securities subject to Section 16 and involving the ValueAct Designee or any member of the ValueAct Group who may be considered a "director by deputization" or who may be deemed to have an indirect interest in the transaction in question.
perturbation.justification: The original text created an obligation to seek board approval. The changed text weakens this to 'consider seeking' board approval. The contradicted text, which contains both the original and changed text, implies that board approval will be sought. The change thus introduces an in-text contradiction as the company no longer has a firm obligation, yet language in the contradicted text still implies that the firm has an obligation to obtain board approval. This aligns with the definition provided: "Ambiguities occur when key terms or obligations are defined inconsistently in different parts of a contract, making enforcement difficult or interpretation unclear."
